title: sterculia bracteata
intro: Sterculia bracteata, also known as the star apple or African star apple, is a tropical tree native to West Africa that belongs to the Sterculiaceae family. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antibacterial effects, which are attributed to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic compounds. The health benefits of Sterculia bracteata include its ability to treat fever, skin conditions, and respiratory issues, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels. In herbal preparations, the leaves, bark, and roots of the plant can be used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, although further research is necessary to confirm its safety and efficacy for human consumption.
